title: Blunt-End-Single-Tube (BEST): Adapter Hybridization
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ff9m","text":"This protocol describes the process of the hybridization of P5 and P7 adapters for the Blunt-End-Single-Tube (BEST) library build protocol. Following this protocol produces the P5+P7 adapter complex for use in the BEST library build. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"90dt5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9rlh8","text":"This protocol is based on: Carøe, C., Gopalakrishnan, S., Vinner, L., T. Mak, S. S., S. Sinding, M. H., Samaniego, J. A., Wales, N., Sicheritz-Pontén, T., \u0026 P. Gilbert, M. T. (2018). Single-tube library preparation for degraded DNA. Methods in Ecology and Evolution, 9(2), 410-419. https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210X.12871","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":233,"length":32},{"style":"italic","offset":267,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e4mhd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"et1ta","text":"Always cite the original paper when using this protocol, in addition to citing this protocols.io protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":7,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"fm3hl","text":"UV-treat aliquots (ddH2O, etc.) for 10 minutes before use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"26ium","text":"Mix all the reagents well, before use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2unft","text":"Do not UV Eppendorf Tubes/PCR tubes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ebdtn","text":"Clean all surfaces with 5% bleach solution followed by 70% ethanol before and after use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8p41e","text":"Clean all equipment with 70% ethanol before and after use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: P5 Adapter (in PCR tube) Reagent Volume 1 IS1 (500μM) 40 μL 2 IS3 (500μM) 40 μL 3 ddH2O 10 μL 4 Oligo hybridization buffer 10 μL 5 Total 100 μL
P7 Adapter (in a different PCR tube to P5) Reagent Volume 1 IS2 (500μM) 40 μL 2 IS3 (500μM) 40 μL 3 ddH2O 10 μL 4 Oligo hybridization buffer 10 μL 5 Total 100 μL
Vortex both tubes & spin down. Make sure they are well mixed.

After cooldown, mix the two adapters in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. This is your final stock tube, so label properly with name (e.g. BEST P5+P7 adapter mix), date, and concentration. This gives a P5+P7 solution of 200 μM .

Prepare the following reagents: Adapters (sequences in table below): IS1 (stock: 500μM) IS2 (stock: 500μM) IS3 (stock: 500μM) Other reagents: Molecular grade H20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #W4502 5M NaCl Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM9760G 1M Tris-HCl, pH 8.0 Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #15568025 (identical to EB buffer Qiagen Catalog #19086 ) 0.5M EDTA Invitrogen Catalog #AM9260G TE Buffer, RNase Free Invitrogen Catalog #AM9858 Table 1 Adapter oligo sequences in 5'-3' direction. Sequences sourced from Carøe et al. (2018) Table S1. Oligo Sequence 1 IS1 5’OMedT*ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT*A 2 IS2 5’OMedT*GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT*A 3 IS3 AGATCGGAAGAGC[C3spacer] *=Phosphothioate linkage All oligos can be synthesized at Biomers, Germany, HPLC purified, shipped dry, and resuspended in 10 mM Tris-HCl.
Prepare the oligo hybridization buffer (10X) Reagent Volume Final Concentration 1 5M NaCl 50 μL 500 mM 2 1M Tris-HCl, pH 8.0 5 μL 10 mM 3 0.5M EDTA 1 μL 1 mM 4 ddH2O 444 μL / 5 Total 500 μL

Incubate in a thermal cycler with a heated lid (105°C) at 95°C for 10s, then ramp down to 12°C at a rate of 0.1°C per second (see table below). Temperature Time 1 95°C 10 sec 2 Ramp down to 12°C 0.1°C per sec 3 10°C Hold

Dilute with TE buffer to the desired working concentration, e.g., 20 μM, 10 μM, 5 μM, 3 μM, 2 μM. The working concentration depends on the input DNA amount, which will be specified in the BEST library build protocol.
creator: Vanssy Li
